title: Expo bets big on React Native’s agentic future - The New Stack
description: Open source app-building platform raises $45M and hires former Meta React lead Seth Webster as it embraces AI coding agents.

title: Digital Experience Monitoring belongs in the modern developer workflow - The New Stack
description: Digital Experience Monitoring connects frontend signals with backend data to help developers ship better user experiences with confidence.

title: WebMCP turns any Chrome web page into an MCP server for AI agents - The New Stack
description: WebMCP turns web pages into MCP servers via a Chrome extension, letting AI agents interact with sites directly. Google and Microsoft back the new standard.

title: Confluent adds A2A support, anomaly detection, and Queues for Kafka in major platform update - The New Stack
description: Confluent adds support for the Agent2Agent protocol, using Apache Kafka to orchestrate inter-agent communication among streaming agents and for anomaly detection.

title: Google's Chrome browser moves to a two-week release cycle - The New Stack
description: Google Chrome will ship new major versions every two weeks starting with version 153 in September 2026, doubling the pace of its current four-week cycle.

title: Meta gave React its own foundation. But it's not letting go just yet. - The New Stack
description: Meta's move of React to a Linux Foundation–backed entity raises questions about control, neutrality, and what makes open source governance transitions succeed.

title: The shift left hangover: Why modern platforms are shifting down to cure developer fatigue - The New Stack
description: The next phase of platform engineering isn't about giving developers more tools; it's about taking requirements off their plate.

title: Mastra empowers web devs to build AI agents in TypeScript - The New Stack
description: Stop letting non-devs have all the fun. Learn agentic AI with Mastra, the open source TypeScript framework for web devs.

title: Drupal turns 25: From simple to complex — then simple again - The New Stack
description: With the release of Drupal CMS 2.0, Drupal marks its 25th year by returning to a simpler content management system — rebuilt for the AI era.

title: Anthropic extends MCP with a UI framework - The New Stack
description: Anthropic is turning Claude into an app platform, with interactive widgets from Slack, Figma, Asana, and others.

title: Distributed apps platform Aspire supports JavaScript, Python - The New Stack
description: In other dev news: Rust updates, a six-month hackathon for useful applications, and a new tool that supports custom AI model benchmarks.

title: Vercel’s json-render: A step toward generative UI - The New Stack
description: A new open source tool brings AI-generated UIs a step closer. Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch tells frontend devs how to prepare.

title: Why platform companies keep buying frontend framework teams - The New Stack
description: The news of Cloudflare acquiring the company behind Astro is just the latest in a string of similar frontend framework deals. We look at the risks and rewards.

title: Astro Redesigns Its Development Server - The New Stack
description: In other dev news, React framework Waku updates its Cloudflare Worker adapter, Svelte fixes vulnerabilities and Bun gets a benchmark.

title: Cloudflare Acquires Team Behind Open Source Framework Astro - The New Stack
description: According to a blog post announcement, the move will let the Astro team “double down” on making Astro the best framework for content-driven websites.

title: StyleX vs. Tailwind: Meta's Take on CSS-in-JS Maintainability - The New Stack
description: Meta's StyleX is a CSS-in-JS solution for large apps. We look at how it compares to Tailwind on code maintainability, scale and atomic CSS.

title: The React Framework Face-Off: Which One Owns the Future? - The New Stack
description: Jack Herrington, aka the Blue Collar Coder, asked framework creators "What's The Framework of the React Future?"

title: Why the Frontend Should Embrace Platform Engineering - The New Stack
description: We look at how platform engineering on the frontend can speed development while maintaining the entire web dev technology stack.

title: Postman Adds Open Source Documentation and SDK Products - The New Stack
description: In other dev news: Good AI code is hard to find, React Router/Remix identifies security issues, and Chrome's new MCP server feature.

title: Tailwind Creator Says AI Played a Role in Downsizing - The New Stack
description: Tailwind CSS is the most popular CSS framework and previously served as an open source success story. Here's why it laid off staff on Monday.

title: A New JavaScript Framework? In this Economy? - The New Stack
description: Sigment is the latest framework to offer a streamlined, "no-build" alternative to the complexity of React. Find out how Sigment simplifies.

title: Why Developers Are Ditching Frameworks for Vanilla JavaScript - The New Stack
description: Frontend developers are returning to Vanilla JavaScript. Here's how native APIs and AI tools are making plain JS the cure for framework fatigue.

title: 2025’s Radical Frontend AI Shift - The New Stack
description: From Netlify’s Agent Runners to the Hashbrown Framework, we look at the innovations that turned the UI into a responsive Gen AI experience.

title: Trends That Defined JavaScript in 2025 - The New Stack
description: Despite AI and the push for simplification in web development, JavaScript frameworks aren’t quite ready to yield their hold on the frontend.

title: Web Components Are the Comeback Nobody Saw Coming - The New Stack
description: Web components enable UI interoperability, allowing you to build reusable components that work across any framework — or to avoid frameworks altogether.

title: TanStack Adds Framework-Agnostic AI Toolkit - The New Stack
description: In other dev news: Anthropic acquires Bun; Rolldown powers Vite 8 beta; and Infragistics open sources Ignite UI for enterprise components.
